prompts.label: ML vs AI vs deep learning
prompts.text:
Define machine learning, artificial intelligence, and deep learning for a beginner.

- Venn-style relationship in plain language
- Supervised vs unsupervised vs reinforcement — one example each
- What you can build with classical ML vs neural nets

No hype — 10 bullets max.

prompts.label: First sklearn classification pipeline
prompts.text:
Build a minimal scikit-learn classification pipeline on the Iris or a toy CSV dataset.

- Load data, train_test_split, StandardScaler, LogisticRegression
- accuracy_score on test set
- Explain fit vs predict in 3 sentences

Runnable Python script under 40 lines.

prompts.label: Train validation test split
prompts.text:
Explain train / validation / test splits and why we need all three.

- Typical ratios and when to use cross-validation instead
- Data leakage examples (scaling on full dataset before split)
- Sketch code with sklearn model_selection

Beginner checklist before trusting metrics.

prompts.label: Confusion matrix and metrics
prompts.text:
Teach confusion matrix, accuracy, precision, recall, F1 for imbalanced classes.

- When accuracy misleads
- sklearn classification_report and confusion_matrix
- Pick a medical/fraud-style example where false negatives hurt

Small synthetic dataset demo.

prompts.label: Overfitting and underfitting
prompts.text:
Explain overfitting and underfitting with learning curves intuition.

- High train accuracy, low test accuracy = overfit
- Regularization teaser (L1/L2, dropout later)
- Plot idea: error vs model complexity — describe what to look for

Suggest 3 practical fixes for overfit.

prompts.label: Feature scaling and encoding
prompts.text:
Preprocess mixed numeric and categorical features for sklearn.

- StandardScaler for numbers, OneHotEncoder for categories
- ColumnTransformer + Pipeline pattern
- Fit only on training data inside pipeline

Complete example with make_column_selector or explicit columns.

prompts.label: Pandas to ML-ready table
prompts.text:
Turn a messy pandas DataFrame into ML-ready features.

- Handle missing values, outliers (IQR or clip), datetime extraction
- Target column y and feature matrix X
- Save cleaned CSV optional

Assume tabular sales or churn-style data.

prompts.label: PyTorch tensor basics
prompts.text:
Introduce PyTorch tensors for someone who knows NumPy.

- Create tensor, shape, dtype, device cpu/cuda mention
- Simple operation and .backward() on scalar — one line why
- Compare torch.tensor vs numpy array conversion

Under 30 lines of code.

prompts.label: Keras sequential model
prompts.text:
Build a tiny Keras Sequential model for MNIST or synthetic regression.

- Dense layers, compile with optimizer and loss
- fit with epochs, evaluate on test
- Mention tf.keras vs standalone keras briefly

Keep training fast (few epochs).

prompts.label: Linear regression intuition
prompts.text:
Explain linear regression as predicting a number from features.

- Line of best fit intuition, MSE loss
- sklearn LinearRegression on one feature — plot slope in comments
- When linear model is enough vs need nonlinear

Math light, intuition heavy.

prompts.label: Logistic regression classifier
prompts.text:
Teach logistic regression for binary classification.

- Sigmoid maps score to probability
- decision boundary at 0.5 threshold
- sklearn LogisticRegression on 2D toy data description

Contrast with linear regression target type.

prompts.label: Random forest when to use
prompts.text:
Explain RandomForestClassifier for tabular data.

- Bagging many trees, feature importance
- Often strong default before deep learning on tables
- Hyperparams: n_estimators, max_depth — what they do

Short code fit on sklearn dataset.

prompts.label: K-fold cross-validation
prompts.text:
Implement k-fold cross-validation with sklearn.

- cross_val_score or StratifiedKFold for classification
- Report mean and std of metric
- When CV beats single holdout split

Use a small public-style dataset name.

prompts.label: Grid search hyperparameters
prompts.text:
Tune hyperparameters with GridSearchCV inside a Pipeline.

- param_grid for model step names
- Avoid leakage: pipeline includes preprocessing
- Show best params and best score only — warn about compute cost

Example with RandomForest or SVM on digits.

prompts.label: Text classification intro NLP
prompts.text:
Classic NLP baseline: TF-IDF + LogisticRegression for text labels.

- CountVectorizer or TfidfVectorizer
- Short list of strings example (spam/ham style)
- Mention transformers as next step, not required now

sklearn Pipeline end to end.

prompts.label: CNN for images overview
prompts.text:
High-level intro to CNNs for image classification.

- Convolution, pooling, fully connected head — roles in bullets
- Transfer learning with pretrained ResNet idea (no full training)
- When to use torchvision/huggingface vs train from scratch

Pseudocode or keras/torch outline only.

prompts.label: Save and load models
prompts.text:
Show how to persist a trained sklearn model and reload for prediction.

- joblib.dump / load
- Warn about pickle security (trusted files only)
- Mention ONNX as interchange format one line

Tiny predict script after load.

prompts.label: Bias fairness checklist
prompts.text:
Give a responsible-ML checklist before shipping a model.

- Representative data, sensitive attributes, disparate impact awareness
- Document limitations and monitoring in production
- Human review for high-stakes decisions

No code — policy and engineering hygiene bullets.

prompts.label: GPU and when you need it
prompts.text:
Explain when GPU helps in ML vs CPU is fine.

- Large neural nets and big batches vs small tabular sklearn
- cuda, device='cuda', cloud notebooks (Colab/Kaggle) mention
- Cost and environment reproducibility tips

Decision table: problem size → hardware.

prompts.label: Learning path sklearn to PyTorch
prompts.text:
Design a 8-week learning path from sklearn tabular ML to PyTorch basics.

- Week themes: pandas → sklearn → metrics → intro NN → pytorch tensors → simple CNN
- Projects per stage (Kaggle Titanic, MNIST)
- Resources types: docs, courses, papers — categories not affiliate links

Realistic hours per week assumption stated.
